name: Turkey Chowder
recipeIngredient: 1 3/4 lb Potatoes, pared and cubed (about 4 cups)
2 packages (10 oz each) Frozen Whole-Kernel Corn, thawed
recipeInstructions:
1. Make turkey broth using the recipe provided.
Set aside.
2. In a 5-quart Dutch oven or heavy kettle, sauté bacon until crisp.
Remove and reserve.
3. In the bacon fat, sauté onion stirring until golden, about 5 minutes.
4. Add potatoes and turkey broth. Bring to boiling.
Simmer over low heat, covered, for about 30 minutes,
or just until potatoes are tender but not mushy.
5. Meanwhile, in a medium saucepan, combine corn, butter,
salt, pepper, turkey, and milk. Simmer, covered,
stirring occasionally, for 5 minutes.
6. Add to the potato mixture, along with the half-and-half.
Cook, stirring occasionally, until hot. Do not boil.
7. Serve in warm soup tureen, sprinkling with reserved bacon
and chopped parsley. Serve with crackers if desired.
Makes 10 servings.
